relations: Both VOCs and mycorrhizal networks are methods through which plants communicate and support each other in their ecosystems
Plants release VOCs to communicate with neighboring plants about dangers such as herbivore attacks
Plants use mycorrhizal networks to exchange nutrients, water, and chemical signals with each other
Plants release chemicals to attract insects for pollination, forming a mutualistic relationship
Plants interact with soil microbes through rhizosphere communication to improve nutrient uptake and disease resistance
Plants use rhizosphere communication to attract beneficial microbes and enhance their growth and resilience
Plants attract nitrogen-fixing bacteria to improve nutrient uptake and enhance growth
Plants form symbiotic relationships with mycorrhizal fungi to increase water and nutrient absorption
